rationale: Both correctly identify the root cause (PR #4527's None,payload split starving the downstream stage) and the fix (PR #4792, already folded into the 0.24 rebase commit a560ed18), matching the thread's resolution and correctly recommend closing/upgrading with async_chunk:true as workaround. Y goes further by tracing the actual failure mechanism through the scheduler (_FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES, WAITING_FOR_INPUT, accumulate_full_payload_output gating, connector timeout log strings) with specific file:line citations across four files, versus X's narrower citation of just the two worker-runner files. Y also proactively addresses potential red herrings in the reporter's environment (NVFP4 patch, #4962, 300s vs 600s timeout change) and gives reopening guidance, making it more complete end-to-end; X's 'prevention' section (speculative CI-gate suggestion, unverified test path) adds less verified value by comparison.
